citation.description: plan_review_health() calls attest.compute_validity() at line 80; audit_trail() calls plan_review_health() at line 199. The audit text renderer (_audit_commands.py:202-243) then prints pin_status with posture label 'enforced' when enforcement is on.

criteria.criterion: Audit rendering calls compute_validity and displays enabled pin invalidity.

citation.description: MCP show_ticket imports plan_review_health from audit.read and assigns it. CLI show (reads_cli.py:187-189), audit trail (read.py:199,217), web page (page.py:397) all consume the same health dict from rebar.audit.read without local status-string definitions.

criteria.criterion: Every detailed renderer consumes DerivedPlanReviewHealth without recreating status strings.

citation.description: _raise_plan_review_close_gate_error() reads the health dict (canonical_id, role, pin_status, enforcement_status, phase, floor) from the gate check result and includes it in the human-readable CommandError. CLI show at reads_cli.py:187-190, MCP at _mcp_reads.py:50-54, audit text/JSON at _audit_commands.py:89-93 and 202-243, web at templates/ticket.html:108-129 all render from the same source.

criteria.criterion: CLI show, MCP detail, audit text/JSON, close errors, and web review UI render the same derived health semantics with canonical IDs and actionable drift reasons.

citation.description: Template renders 'advisory; enforcement disabled' when enforcement_disabled and advisory=True. CLI audit commands (_audit_commands.py:211-219) and close errors (transition_close.py:54-60) use identical logic. Test test_renderer_keeps_legacy_unpinned_distinct (test_plan_review_health_surfaces.py:185-198) verifies legacy-unpinned is not rewritten as 'current (no related material)'. plan_review_health is added only in the show arm of reads_cli.py (lines 172-189), not in the list arm.

criteria.criterion: Enforcement-off drift is visibly advisory, legacy-unpinned is distinguishable, and ordinary list output plus persisted events remain unchanged.

citation.description: ticket_state.schema.json defines the plan_review_health property with pin_status, phase_status, targets, enforcement_status, advisory, signed_phase, required_phase, effective_execution_floor. Rendering tests in test_plan_review_health_surfaces.py cover all surfaces. docs/plan-review-gate.md lines 873-895 cover pins/phases/enforcement toggles/deleted-target repair (stale-pin-missing)/close-vs-claim freshness.

criteria.criterion: Output schemas, rendering tests, and advanced documentation cover pins, phases, toggles, deleted targets, and close-versus-claim freshness.
